              `totalDistanceKm: p.computedDistanceKm ?? p.totalDistanceKm`.
              Prisma query at L305-319 uses `include:` so computedDistanceKm
              (added by a470e7a, populated by 2dad7a4) already returned on
              `p` with no select change. CoverageReportPatrolRow interface
              field name unchanged (still `totalDistanceKm` as the output
              key) — fallback resolves at the mapper layer so downstream
              consumers (print-render pages, PDF template) keep working
              unchanged. When PatrolTrack materialize has run, Coverage
              Report shows actual haversine-summed GPS distance; when not
              yet recomputed (legacy patrols, patrols with no PatrolTrack
              row), `??` falls back to totalDistanceKm preserving prior
              behavior. Null-safe both ways. 2 files +69/-1: source L334
              (1-line flip), test file +68L with 2 new it blocks in
              existing getCoverageReportData describe — "prefers
              computedDistanceKm when both present" (computed=12.5
              total=8.4 expect 12.5) + "falls back to totalDistanceKm
              when computed null" (computed=null total=8.4 expect 8.4).

              ?? totalDistanceKm falls back identically to null ??
              totalDistanceKm, so legacy tests continue passing without
              churn — kept diff minimal). Web suite 730 → 732 (+2).

              recomputeDistanceAndDuration(prisma, patrolId) in
              patrol-track-materialization.ts haversine-sums PatrolTrack
              trackGeojson coordinates pairwise within each LineString feature
              (no endpoint-connecting across features — actual GPS movement
              only), computes duration = abs(max-min) over all
              coordinateProperties.times in hours, writes
              Patrol.computedDistanceKm + computedDurationHours via
              prisma.patrol.update. No-ops gracefully when PatrolTrack row
              absent or trackGeojson empty (computed_* stay null —
              "not yet computed" vs "zero"). Same positional-client + no-tx
              convention as materializePatrolTrack (idempotent, concurrent-
              safe). Wired into processPatrolTrackMaterialize at L66 —
              invoked after materializePatrolTrack resolves, gated on
              !result.skipped (skip guard matters: materialize can
              short-circuit on no_credentials/no_segment/no_leader without
              a trackGeojson to sum). Activates 2 of 12 Patrol v2 additive
              columns from a470e7a (computed_distance_km +
              computed_duration_hours). Combined with sync mapper 1f1238f
              (4 columns auto-filled on ER sync), 6 of 12 a470e7a columns
              now populated. 4 files +345/-6 net: lib +110L (helper + private
              haversineKm + RecomputeResult interface), lib test +206L
              (extended MockPrisma factory with patrol.update +
              patrolTrack.findUnique per memory feedback rule, 6 new tests
              covering single-feature haversine + multi-feature non-connecting
              sum + missing row + empty trackGeojson + missing times +
              Patrol.update side-effect), processor +7L (import + 4-line
              conditional call replacing 1-line return), processor test +28L
              (extended vi.mock + 2 integration tests). Jobs suite 132 → 140
              (+8). Phase 7 HARD PRE-MERGE GATE 4/4 GREEN — product-sync ✓,
              typecheck 7/7 (cached 5/7), web 730/730 unchanged, jobs 140,
              web build clean (29/29 static pages, ESLint clean). 1 Sonnet
              Scout (R5 — patrol-track-materialization.ts + processor +
              schema, returned function signature + integration point + test
              patterns) + 2 Sonnet Implementer dispatches (A2.1 helper +
              helper tests, A2.2 processor wire + integration tests). V32
              R1 honored — Opus wrote only this STATE.md + commit message.
              Closes Patrol v2 consumer loop one step further; Coverage
              Report flip to prefer computed_distance_km is the next
              immediate move (separate ship — keeps tests scoped).

              pdf-render Mode B harness (2026-06-02 00:48 GMT+8 — commit 7efbe3a):

                Goal: third Mode B verification harness — generalize the BullMQ-
                state-polling pattern across 3 distinct queues (area-rederive,
                patrol-track-materialize, now pdf-render). Closes verification
                coverage on the funder-facing PDF pipeline (Coverage Report +
                Per Area Report). Catches RLS/FK/Valkey-hop/worker-pickup/
                pdf-renderer-connection/MinIO-upload bugs that unit tests
                fundamentally cannot.

                Implementation (~303L net, 1 new file, 1 Sonnet dispatch — Tier 1):
                  scripts/verify-pdf-render.ts (NEW, 303L):
                    Section 1 — Load .env.dev (no dotenv dep) — copied verbatim
                      from sibling harness template.
                    Section 2 — CLI args: --tenantId (default cmoruubw20000gmx3jx7zudmy
                      demo-site), --reportType (area | coverage, default area),
                      --paperSize (A4 | LETTER, default A4), --timeout-ms
                      (default 60000 — Puppeteer is slower than patrol-track).
                    Section 3 — Workspace imports: enqueuePdfRender,
                      getPdfRenderQueue, closeAllQueues, platformPrisma.
                    Section 4 — Local types: RenderResult structural copy.
                    Section 5 — Snapshot helpers: fetchReportExportSnapshot,
                      fmtSnapshot, describeChange.
                    Section 6 — Main:
                      1. Resolve first super_admin user.id (requestedById).
                      2. Insert fresh ReportExport row via platformPrisma.
                         reportExport.create with status="queued", paramsJson={}.
                      3. Print BEFORE snapshot.
                      4. Enqueue pdf-render with the new exportId.
                      5. Poll BullMQ state every 500ms until completed/failed
                         /timeout. Print state transitions inline (`waiting →
                         active → completed` style).
                      6. On completed: log returnvalue JSON, verify
                         returnvalue.status === "ready", refetch ReportExport,
                         verify status === "ready" + filePath set → PASS.
                      7. On failed: log failedReason → FAIL.
                      8. On timeout: FAIL with last-state context.
                      9. Print AFTER snapshot regardless of outcome.
                    Section 7 — Catch FATAL: cleanup + exit 1.

              Option B — user.updateRole hardening (2026-06-01 23:25 GMT+8 — commit 652d33d):

                Goal: close the load-bearing RBAC gap in apps/web/src/server/trpc/
                routers/user.ts L150-162. Pre-ship behavior used `prisma.user.
                updateMany({ where: { id, tenantId: ctx.tenantId }, ... })` which
                silently returned `count: 0` when a site_admin attempted to update
                a user belonging to another tenant. Caller could not distinguish
                "user not found" from "cross-tenant attempt" from "success", no
                FORBIDDEN/NOT_FOUND signal, no audit row written, return shape
                `{ count }` leaked zero-update state. Mirror the platformUser.
                updateRole hardening pattern (L169-249) — findFirst → throw →
                update → writeAuditLog.

                Implementation (~80L net across 2 files, 1 module — Tier 1, both
                files under 200L so no V32 R3 line ranges required):

                  apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/user.ts (181L → 204L, +23L net):
                    +import writeAuditLog from @marine-guardian/db
                    +import PrismaClient type from @marine-guardian/db
                    L150-162 updateRole body rewritten:
                      1. findFirst { where: { id, tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
                         select: { id, role, tenantId } }
                      2. if (!existing) throw TRPCError NOT_FOUND
                         (same message — no enumeration leak between
                         "user does not exist" vs "user exists in other tenant")
                      3. prisma.user.update { where: { id }, data: { role,
                         securityVersion: { increment: 1 } } }
                      4. writeAuditLog(prisma as unknown as PrismaClient, {
                           tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId,
                           action: "UPDATE_USER_ROLE", entityType: "User",
                           entityId: input.id,
                           changesJson: { before: { role: existing.role },
                                          after: { role: input.role } },
                           ipAddress: ctx.ip })
                      5. return { id: input.id }  (new shape; was { count })

                  apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/user.test.ts
                  (160L → 217L, +57L net):
                    +import writeAuditLog mock
                    Mock factory extended: user.findFirst + user.update added to shape
                    +describe("user.updateRole", ...) with 2 new cases:
                      - "throws NOT_FOUND when target user belongs to a different
                         tenant": findFirst returns null → assert TRPCError code
                         NOT_FOUND, assert prisma.user.update NOT called, assert
                         writeAuditLog NOT called
                      - "updates role and writes audit log on success": findFirst
                         returns existing user, update succeeds, assert
                         writeAuditLog called with UPDATE_USER_ROLE + before/after
                         role + tenantId from ctx

                  ✅ pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/web build (Next.js prod build clean)

              PatrolTrack Materialization live QA (2026-06-01 19:22 GMT+8 — commit ce6e5cd):

                Goal: live-verify the patrol-track-materialize worker pipeline against
                real Valkey + Postgres, completing the deferred-enqueue verification
                coverage for the second job type (first was area-rederive, 746277c).
                Same long-term-leverage philosophy as G2b — establish a reusable
                template for any future deferred-job harness.

                Approach (Mode B chosen — BullMQ job-state polling, not Postgres):
                  Postgres polling fails for PatrolTrack because the worker may
                  legitimately skip without writing — dev tenants have no ER
                  credentials and dev patrols have 0 segments, so every job hits
                  the no_segment / no_credentials precondition guard and returns
                  skipped=true. Mode B polls queue.getJob(jobId).getState() until
                  the state reaches "completed" or "failed". A clean skip with
                  reason is still a successful pipeline traversal. The job's
                  returnvalue (MaterializationResult) documents WHY no write
                  occurred. This generalizes to ANY deferred job — pdf-render,
                  alerts, email — not just Postgres writers.

                Result (first run, demo-site Active Surveillance patrol cmpqv0d4l000ygmgssbnrmt9x):
                  BEFORE: patrolTrackId=null
                  state: completed
                  returnvalue: { skipped: true, skipReason: "no_segment",
                                 patrolTrackId: null, pointCount: 0,
                                 hasTimestamps: false, lastTrackTime: null,
                                 patrolEnded: false }
                  AFTER:  patrolTrackId=null (unchanged — no row)
                  PASS in 502ms — matches area-rederive baseline (503ms).
                  Pipeline confirmed: enqueue → Valkey → worker pickup →
                  Prisma load → return MaterializationResult.

                Pre-verification state of dev (already mapped during G2b scoping):
                  - Demo Site + QA Test Reef both have ER URL + DAS token NULL
                  - 4 patrols seeded for Demo Site (none have started)
                  - 0 rows in patrol_segments — every patrol hits no_segment
                  - 0 rows in patrol_tracks — no prior materialization
                  - All 7 workers including patrol-track-materialize registered

                Gate (HARD PRE-MERGE 4-cmd, all exit 0):
                  pnpm tools:check-product-sync             ✓
                  pnpm typecheck                            7/7 ✓
                  pnpm test                                710/710 web + cached ✓
                  pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/web build   ✓

                V32 R1 honored: 1 Sonnet dispatch (D1 harness write, 286L single
                file create). Sonnet tool use: 7 (slight overshoot vs V32.1 ≤5

                feat (commit 3794c10) — AreaBoundary derivation algorithm:
                  Wired enqueueAreaRederive in er-sync.processor.ts after every
                  Event AND Patrol upsert. ER payload contains no areaName field
                  (per memory ingest 2984) — derivation falls back to
                  nearest-boundary via lat/lon for Events (which have coords);
                  Patrols use name-only match (no coords) and return null if
                  unmatched. Closes the deferred 5.1d Area A item parked in
                  queues/types.ts L51-54.

                  enqueueAlert (er-sync.processor.ts:196). queues/types.ts L40-41
                  schema docs anticipated this wiring as "sync-system sentinel
                  for automatic enqueues". applyAreaDerivation explicitly skips
                  AuditLog writes for automatic contexts (area-derivation.ts
                  L16-19), so sentinel never hits the User FK.
                  Restructured syncEvents to thread `eventId` through both
                  create + update branches — area-rederive enqueues once per
                  iteration regardless of path. Alert enqueue semantics preserved
                  (still only on create per tests L169 + L185).

LAST_DONE:    Coverage Report computed_distance_km adoption — flips Coverage
              Report mapper to prefer Patrol.computedDistanceKm over
              Patrol.totalDistanceKm when present. Shipped commit 273635c
              on local main (2dad7a4..273635c). First v2 column CONSUMER
              (read-side) — 2dad7a4 was the writer; this ship makes the
              column visible end-to-end in operator-facing reports.

              apps/web/src/server/coverage-report/get-coverage-report-data.ts
              L334 single-line flip from `totalDistanceKm: p.totalDistanceKm`
              to `totalDistanceKm: p.computedDistanceKm ?? p.totalDistanceKm`.
              Prisma findMany at L305-319 uses `include:` (not `select:`),
              so computedDistanceKm — added by a470e7a schema and populated
              by 2dad7a4 recompute helper — is already returned on `p` with
              zero select change required. CoverageReportPatrolRow interface
              output key stays `totalDistanceKm` (fallback resolves
              internally) — downstream consumers (print-render Page 1/2/3
              templates, PDF generator) keep working unchanged. Null-safe
              both ways: computed=null AND total=null → result stays null
              (same as today, no breakage).

              Test surface: web 730 → 732 (+2) — 2 new it blocks added in
              existing `describe("getCoverageReportData")`:
              (a) "prefers computedDistanceKm over totalDistanceKm when
              both present" (computed=12.5, total=8.4 → expect 12.5);
              (b) "falls back to totalDistanceKm when computedDistanceKm
              is null" (computed=null, total=8.4 → expect 8.4). Existing
              11 patrol mocks NOT updated — per Scout B1 analysis,
              `undefined ?? p.totalDistanceKm` falls back identically to
              `null ?? p.totalDistanceKm`, so legacy tests pass without
              churn. Kept diff minimal: 1 source line + 1 test describe
              addition.

LAST_DONE_PRIOR_SESSIONS: (preserved for audit trail — Executed Option C +
              prior multi-ship narrative below; new narrative blocks at the
              top of this file are authoritative for the current state.)

              Executed Option C per prior STATE.md NEXT recommendation.
              Two-step ship with one revert mid-flight:

              STEP 1 — feat 5dfa8f5 (Sonnet dispatch 1):
                apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/patrolSchedule.ts (+53/-1)
                  - Added `writeAuditLog` to the @marine-guardian/db
                    import (existing `prisma` import on L6).
                  - `create` mutation: when `input.overrideConflicts ===
                    true`, after the existing conflict-check block,
                    do the create then write an AuditLog row with
                    action="PATROL_SCHEDULE:OVERRIDE_CONFLICT",
                    entityType="PatrolSchedule", entityId=created.id,
                    changesJson={rangerUserId, scheduledStart,
                    scheduledEnd}. Non-override path unchanged.
                  - `update` mutation: same pattern — when override
                    flag set, do the update then write audit row with
                    the resolved data field as changesJson (Dates
                    converted to ISO strings).
                apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/patrolSchedule.test.ts (+46/-1)
                  - Mocked writeAuditLog at module level.
                  - 2 existing override tests now assert
                    `writeAuditLog` was called with the correct
                    action string.
                  - 2 new tests assert NO audit write on non-override
                    success paths (create + update).
                docs/CHANGELOG_AI.md (+9/-0) — entry prepended.

                HARD GATE results: typecheck 0 errors → 707/707 vitest
                passing → `pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/web build`
                clean. All three Phase 7 Step 19 commands exit 0.

                Tradeoff: writeAuditLog called sequentially after
                mutation (non-atomic). Argument cast as
                `prisma as unknown as any` because the tenant-guarded
                extended client doesn't trivially satisfy the helper's
                `PrismaClient | TransactionClient` signature.

              STEP 2 — refactor 7853f18 (Sonnet dispatch 2):
                Intended to wrap both override branches in
                `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => ...)` to make the
                create+audit atomic and remove the `as any` cast.
                Sonnet instead switched to `platformPrisma.$transaction`
                — citing platform.ts L281 as precedent.

              STEP 3 — REVERT 7a11af8 (Opus, via git revert):
                platformPrisma is the BARE PrismaClient with NO
                extensions (packages/db/src/client.ts L23):
                  `export const platformPrisma = new PrismaClient();`
                while `prisma` is `.$extends(encryption).$extends(
                tenantGuardExtension)`.

                Using platformPrisma inside coordinatorProcedure
                (tenant-scoped, ctx.tenantId session-derived) BYPASSES
                the L6 Prisma guardrail extension on the override-
                mutation path only. This violates:
                  - Rule 7 L6 — "always active" tenant guardrails
                  - security.md "SUPERADMIN AND PLATFORM-LEVEL ROLES":
                    "Superadmin queries that bypass tenant scoping
                    MUST use a dedicated Prisma client instance
                    WITHOUT the L6 tenant-guard extension ...
                    Regular tenant-scoped routers continue using the
                    guarded prisma instance."

                platform.ts L281 is the legitimate use of
                platformPrisma: it CREATES a brand-new tenant + admin
                from a superadmin context (tenantId=null on the
                audit row). patrolSchedule mutations are tenant-scoped
                operations on existing tenants — they MUST use
                tenant-guarded prisma.

                Reverted via `git revert --no-edit 7853f18`. Working
                tree at 7a11af8 is byte-identical to 5dfa8f5
                (`git diff 5dfa8f5..HEAD --stat` = empty).

              FINAL STATE: 5dfa8f5 content on main, with revert
              commit 7a11af8 on top. Tests + lint + build all pass.
              Option C functional — every override-flag mutation now
              writes a PATROL_SCHEDULE:OVERRIDE_CONFLICT audit row
              with the correct tenant guard intact.

              KNOWN RESIDUAL CODE SMELL (acceptable):
                `writeAuditLog(prisma as unknown as any, {...})` —
                two occurrences in patrolSchedule.ts override paths
                (L138, L221 region). Not a security issue. Could be
                tightened to `as unknown as PrismaClient` if revisited.

              KNOWN RESIDUAL BEHAVIOR (acceptable):
                Non-atomic create→audit on override path. If the audit
                INSERT fails after the schedule INSERT succeeds, the
                override happens without audit trail. Same DB
                connection / immediate sequential write makes this
                vanishingly unlikely in practice. A future polish dispatch
                could wrap in `prisma.$transaction` (the tenant-guarded
                instance — NOT platformPrisma) if the cast can be
                resolved cleanly; needs investigation of why Sonnet
                couldn't make the tx type match writeAuditLog's signature
                without escaping to platformPrisma.
